message:
feat(stdlib): add string representation receipt tracking with Unicode observability

Introduce StringRepresentationKind, StringRepresentationReceipt, and
StringMethodResult types to track how string operations select their
internal representation (Inline, Flat, SliceView, RopeCandidate).

Key additions:
- STRING_INLINE_CHAR_MAX (22) and STRING_FLATTEN_BUDGET_CODE_UNITS (256)
  constants controlling representation selection thresholds
- build_string_representation_receipt() computes Unicode observability
  metrics: UTF-16 code unit counts, BMP vs non-BMP detection, ASCII flags,
  segment counts, and flatten budget tracking for each string operation
- exec_string_method_with_receipt() wraps exec_string_method to optionally
  emit a receipt when the result is a string value
- classify_string_representation() selects Inline/Flat/SliceView/RopeCandidate
  based on char length, flatten cost, and segment count

Integration tests verify:
- Unicode slice receipts track non-BMP emoji correctly
- Concat operations report flatten budget pressure and exhaustion
- Full artifact triad scenario (slice + concat + repeat) produces
  coherent receipts with cross-validated metrics

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
